firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: NSW FHBAS: homes (including eligible off-the-plan homes) $0 duty up to $800,000, concession to $1,000,000; vacant land $0 duty up to $350,000, concession to $450,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: First Home Buyers Assistance Scheme — full transfer duty exemption applies for eligible homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, up to $800,000, or vacant land up to $350,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: First Home Buyers Assistance Scheme — concessional transfer duty applies for eligible homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, from $800,001 to $1,000,000, or vacant land from $350,001 to $450,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No FHBAS concession applies above the current home or vacant land thresholds. Standard transfer duty applies.

grant.name: NSW FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for a newly built, eligible off-the-plan, or substantially renovated home up to $600,000, or for vacant land plus an eligible building contract up to $750,000. Vacant land eligibility depends on the combined land-and-build cost, so treat the app result as an estimate only.

state.mortgageFeeNotes: Paper fee is $135.80. Electronic mortgage registration and discharge fees are treated here as $125.70 each.
state.federalNotes: Victoria also has separate off-the-plan concession pathways, including a temporary concession for some contracts entered from 21 October 2024 to 20 October 2026.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: VIC first home buyer duty relief: $0 duty up to $600,000, concession to $750,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: First Home Buyer exemption — $0 land transfer duty (property ≤ $600 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: First Home Buyer concession — concessional (sliding scale) duty applies ($600 001–$750 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No First Home Buyer concession (property > $750 000). Standard duty applies.

grant.name: Victorian FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for new homes, including eligible off-the-plan purchases, ≤ $750 000. Must never have previously owned residential property in Australia. Occupy as PPR within 12 months for at least 12 continuous months.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: QLD first home duty relief: established homes $0 duty up to $700,000, concession to $800,000; eligible new homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, and vacant land can receive full relief.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: Queensland First Home concession or exemption applies. Eligible new homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, and eligible vacant land can receive full duty relief under the current first home concessions.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: Queensland First Home concession applies on a partial basis for established homes from $700,001 to $800,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No Queensland First Home concession applies for this property value or property type.

grant.name: QLD FHOG ($30k)
grant.notes: $30 000 for eligible first home buyers buying or building a new home in Queensland, including an eligible off-the-plan home, where the total value is under $750,000. This increased amount applies until 30 June 2026.

grant.name: QLD FHOG ($15k)
grant.notes: $15 000 for eligible first home buyers buying or building a new home in Queensland, including an eligible off-the-plan home, where the total value is under $750,000.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: WA first home duty relief: homes (including eligible off-the-plan homes) $0 duty up to $500,000, concession to $700,000 in metro / Peel or $750,000 in regional WA; vacant land $0 duty up to $350,000, concession to $450,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: WA First Home Owner Rate of Duty applies. Homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, can receive a full transfer duty exemption up to $500,000, and vacant land up to $350,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: WA First Home Owner Rate of Duty applies on a concessional basis above the exemption threshold and within the current metro / Peel, regional, or vacant land ranges.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: WA First Home Owner Rate of Duty does not apply above the current home or vacant land thresholds. Standard transfer duty applies.

grant.name: WA FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for new homes, including eligible off-the-plan homes, south of the 26th parallel (≤ $750 000). $10 000 for new homes north of the 26th parallel (≤ $1 000 000). Only new or substantially renovated homes qualify. Must occupy as PPR within 12 months for at least 6 months.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: SA first home duty relief: $0 duty on eligible new homes, eligible off-the-plan apartments, and vacant land with no price cap; no concession for established homes.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: SA First Home Buyer stamp duty exemption — $0 duty on a new home, eligible off-the-plan apartment, or vacant land (no price cap, from 6 June 2024).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: SA First Home Buyer — partial concession does not apply in SA.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: SA provides NO stamp duty concession on established homes. Full duty applies regardless of buyer status.

grant.name: SA FHOG
grant.notes: $15 000 for new homes, including eligible off-the-plan apartments — NO property value cap (from 6 June 2024). Must never have owned residential property in Australia. Occupy as PPR for at least 6 continuous months commencing within 12 months of settlement. A propertyCap of -1 indicates no cap.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: TAS first home duty relief: $0 duty on eligible established homes up to $750,000 between 18 February 2024 and 30 June 2026.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: Tasmania established-home duty exemption applies: $0 duty for an eligible first home buyer purchasing an established home valued at $750,000 or less, settling between 18 February 2024 and 30 June 2026.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: Tasmania established-home duty relief has no taper. Above $750,000, full standard duty applies.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No Tasmania established-home duty exemption applies for this property value or date.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($30k)
grant.notes: $30,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 April 2021 and 30 June 2024. Applies to a new home, off-the-plan home, or eligible build transaction. Must satisfy Tasmania First Home Owner Grant eligibility rules.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($10k transitional)
grant.notes: $10,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 July 2024 and 30 June 2025. This transitional period does not require the higher $30,000-specific building criteria.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($30k current)
grant.notes: $30,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 July 2025 and 30 June 2026. Applies to a new home, off-the-plan home, or eligible build transaction and must satisfy Tasmania First Home Owner Grant eligibility rules.

state.federalNotes: In the ACT, duty is paid 14 days after registration, not at settlement.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: ACT HBCS: income-tested $0 duty up to $1,020,000, concession to $1,455,000.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: ACT Home Buyer Concession Scheme — $0 conveyance duty (≤ $1 020 000). Income test applies (household taxable income limit); must not have owned property in last 5 years. Check revenue.act.gov.au for current income thresholds.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: ACT Home Buyer Concession Scheme — partial concession applies ($1 020 001–$1 455 000, max saving $35 238). Income and ownership tests apply.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: ACT HBCS does not apply (property > $1 455 000 or eligibility criteria not met). Standard conveyance duty payable.

grant.name: ACT HBCS (informational only)
grant.notes: ACT no longer has a cash First Home Owner Grant. The Home Buyer Concession Scheme is already modelled in the duty calculation and should not be added as a separate cash grant.

state.mortgageFeeNotes: Add $69.00 for each additional title on registration or discharge.

grant.name: HomeGrown Territory Grant (Established Home)
grant.notes: $10,000 for eligible first home buyers purchasing an established home in the NT, subject to current NT Treasury eligibility rules. There is no property value cap. This established-home payment ends on 30 September 2025.

grant.name: HomeGrown Territory Grant (New Home)
grant.notes: $50,000 for eligible first home buyers buying or building a new home in the NT under HomeGrown Territory, subject to current NT Treasury eligibility rules. There is no property value cap. Current scheme dates run to 30 September 2026.

grant.name: House and Land Package Exemption
grant.notes: Separate NT house-and-land package relief can provide full stamp duty exemption for eligible package purchases until 30 June 2027. Confirm current NT Treasury eligibility rules before relying on it.
